text: The lateral lymph nodes, showing green fluorescence, were located.
After the standard procedure of TME, the LLND stage started.
Firstly, the peritoneum on the outside of the medial umbilical fold was opened.
Then,
The ureterohypogastric nerve fascia was exposed to establish the medial boundary for nodal dissection.
Under the guidance of ICG fluorescence imaging, it is simple to identify the space between lymph nodes and ureterohypogastric nerve fascia.
The ureter and the hypogastric nerve included in it were preserved as a single membrane.
Next, we began to dissect the parietal pelvic fascia to establish the lateral boundary for nodal dissection.
During the nodal dissection, we focused on the internal iliac region and obturator region due to a high frequency of metastasis.
So, we opened the fascia on the medial side of the external iliac vessels instead of the lateral side to spare the external and common iliac region.
The external iliac artery and vein were exposed successively.

under the guidance of icg fluorescence imaging the lymphatic adipose tissues in a traitor region were separated from the medial aspect of external iliac vessels the umbilical artery was exposed which formed the boundary between obturator nodes and internal iliac nodes
Dissecting along the external iliac vessel caudally, the iliopsos muscle and the obturator internus muscle were exposed successively.
Thus, the lateral boundary of nodal dissection was established.
Next, we turn to the dissection of the vesico-hypogastric fascia.
Through the dissection of the anatomic level between the vesico-hypogastric fascia and obturator lymph nodes, the retzius space was extended cordially.
And so far, the three facial spaces were fully exposed, including uretero-hypogastric nerve fascia, vesico-hypogastric fascia, and pelvic fascia.
The abjurator nodes and internal iliac nodes were enclosed in this region, and divided by the umbilical artery.
Then we began to dissect the proximal part of the internal iliac lymph nodes.
There, as a distinct boundary line between the abjurator lymph nodes, and internal iliac lymph nodes,
continued with the vesico-hypogastric fascia.
Dissection continued along the visceral branch of the internal iliac artery.
The superior vesicle artery was discovered to originate from the umbilical artery.
Both of them were preserved carefully.
The internal iliac artery and vein were dissected cordially.
The proximal part of internal iliac lymph nodes was removed.
Then, the obturator lymph node dissection began.
Dissection continued along the internal iliac artery.
The obturator artery was isolated and transected at the root.

Dissecting the confluence of the internal iliac artery and the external iliac artery, the proximal part of the obturator nerve was identified and bluntly dissected out of the lymphatic adipose tissues.
The obturator vein was isolated and transected at the root.
Another obturator vein was also transected.
Then the obturator region was dissected along the obturator internus muscle fascia.
Obturator vessels were isolated at the level of the obturator foramen entry point.
The obturator artery was transected from the distal side.
The obturator nerve was bluntly dissected out of the lymphatic adipose tissues.
The obturator vein was also transected from the distal side, with the obturator nerve protected carefully.
Then, the obturator lymph nodes were removed.
Then we began to dissect the distal part of internal iliac lymph nodes.
It presents a certain degree of challenge due to its deep location and complex anatomical structures.
With dissection near the bladder wall, the inferior vesicle artery and vein were exposed and preserved carefully under the guidance of ICG fluorescence imaging.
Dissection continued quarterly to the tendinous arch of pelvic fascia.
Then a window was opened to the TME compartment to connect the two spaces.
The distal part of the internal iliac nodes was removed completely, with the inferior vesicle vessels spared.
Here showed the preserved vesicle vessels after nodal dissection.
The obturator artery and obturator vein were transected, with the obturator nerve protected well.
The umbilical artery and the superior vesicle artery originated from it were both preserved.
The inferior vesicle artery and vein were isolated and preserved as well.
Here's the final field of view.
